note: Cold spec-judge round 1 returned ALIGNED false — builder lens FAIL. Every drafted scenario asserted the guard fails closed; none asserted it raises no violation on input that parses. An implementation hardcoded to always fail closed would satisfy the whole suite, so the corpus sweep's new contract could not distinguish measuring from refusing. This is the safety dual of the fail-open defect the CR exists to close. Closed additively with two discriminating scenarios (clean corpus raises no parse violation and does not fail closed; a parsing touched file raises none).

Round 1 architect observation prompted a re-read that refuted the observation and found a prose defect instead. The README claimed the parse guard fails the tree-wide sweep closed unlike the touched-scope checks around it. There is no such asymmetry: the form check already fails the tree-wide sweep closed today, which is why the corpus check gates CI. Prose corrected to state the guard takes the same path as every other form violation, distinguished only by which parser renders the verdict.
